title: SoftSkills
description: Canonical 9-attribute soft-skill taxonomy applied to every character regardless of profession. Soft skills compose with profession-specific hard skills (industry.schema.json professions[].base_skills[]) into character ranking via professions[].overall_rating_formula. Soft skills evolve slowly (typically 0.5-1.5 points per in-world year) and are mostly cap-bounded by personality archetype, distinguishing them from coachable hard skills which can move fast under training. Substrate engine ticks soft-skill nudges in response to life events (won title, contract dispute resolved, public scandal), not weekly drills. Per skills-design doctrine — see polis/SKILLS-DESIGN.md.

composure.description: Performance under pressure. High composure → footballer takes the penalty in 89th minute and hits the corner; manager fronts the press after a 4-0 home defeat and stays on-message. Low composure → mistakes in big moments, defensive answers in interviews.

charisma.description: Influence on other characters in social and broadcast contexts. Modulates relationship-building speed, dressing-room presence, supporter affinity, broadcasting performance for journalist roles.

resilience.description: Bounce-back from setback. Affects post-injury return, post-relegation rebuild, post-public-scandal recovery, repeat-failure-then-succeed arcs. Modulates how quickly mood and form recover after negative events.

integrity.description: Honesty and consistency. Affects propensity to honour contracts vs angle for transfer, propensity to disclose to media vs spin, susceptibility to bribery / match-fixing approaches (low integrity → higher risk surface). Substrate hard floor (Constitution Article XI) refuses CSAM / real-world-threats regardless of this value — integrity governs only legal-but-shady behaviours.

decisiveness.description: Speed under uncertainty. Affects tactical adjustments mid-match (manager), career-moment decisions (transfer offer with tight window), institution-level choices (board votes). High decisiveness → faster ping-resolution by the character without escalating to player; low decisiveness → more frequent ping_player by default.

empathy.description: Sensitivity to others' states. Affects management of dressing-room conflict, agent-client trust, mentor-relationships, ability to handle players going through personal difficulty. High empathy unlocks mentor-bonus to younger characters' growth_curve.

ambition.description: Drive to advance. Affects job-application willingness for stretch roles, willingness to accept bigger contracts at lower-quality clubs vs smaller contracts at bigger clubs, retirement timing. High ambition → frequent stretch-application; low ambition → settles for current role.

ego.description: Self-regard. Affects acceptance of squad-rotation, willingness to share credit, response to public criticism. High ego compounds with low integrity to produce difficult dressing-room characters; high ego with high integrity produces principled stars who clash on principle. Distinct from ambition — ambition is forward-pointed; ego is self-pointed.

work_ethic.description: Daily effort. Modulates effective training_load_per_week multiplier on hard-skill growth_curves: a player with work_ethic 90 gains skills at ~1.2× the curve rate; work_ethic 30 gains at ~0.7×. Work-ethic itself improves slowly via mentor relationships (high-empathy mentor + young mentee) and degrades after sustained demoralising periods (relegation, contract dispute, locker-room poison).
